title: Stator output voltage verification and replacement
description: Factory 38-amp stator on Twin Cam 96/103 Dyna models fails prematurely due to heat buildup and insulation breakdown around the stator windings, causing undercharging and eventual no-charge condition. The Dyna platform's exposed oil-cooled stator runs hotter than Softail or Touring variants because the frame geometry limits airflow across the primary case.
fix_procedure: Test stator AC output per Service Manual Chapter 7: measure across each of the three stator leads with engine at 2000 rpm. Output must be 38-52 VAC between any two leads. Replace stator (part 29965-06A) if any reading falls below 32 VAC. Inspect rotor magnets for debris. Torque stator bolts to 84 in-lbs. Apply Loctite 243. Primary fluid capacity 32 oz of Formula+ transmission lubricant.
verified_by: HD Service Bulletin M-1287 Rev A

title: Fuel tank vent hose routing correction
description: 2014-2020 Touring models may develop fuel starvation at highway speeds caused by a pinched fuel tank vent hose routed beneath the tank shell. Symptom presents as intermittent stumble above 65 mph and engine stall during deceleration. Contributing factor is OEM routing through a mounting bracket that compresses the vent hose under vibration load.
fix_procedure: Remove fuel tank per Service Manual Chapter 4. Reroute vent hose (part 62308-08) along the frame spine, securing with cable ties at 6-inch intervals. Do not route through bracket 61351-08. Verify vent opens to atmosphere at the airbox breather point. Labor time: 1.3 hours. Test ride 15 minutes after repair to confirm resolution.

title: ECM reflash for cold-start hesitation on Milwaukee-Eight 107
description: Milwaukee-Eight 107 powered Softail models built between 2018 and late 2020 may exhibit cold-start stumbling, backfire through intake, and rough idle below 50°F ambient. Root cause identified as conservative fuel-enrichment table in pre-03-2021 ECM firmware. Affected VIN range published in bulletin appendix.
fix_procedure: Connect Digital Technician II or Dynojet Power Vision. Verify current calibration against bulletin table. If below revision 1.07.19.00, update to 1.09.12.00 or later. Reflash duration: 12 minutes. Disconnect battery for 60 seconds after flash. Relearn idle by cycling ignition three times without starting, then idle for 10 minutes at 800 rpm without throttle input. Warranty code 30A.

title: ABS Hydraulic Control Unit replacement for pressure loss
description: ABS equipped Touring models may develop a brake pressure fade condition caused by internal seal failure in the HCU solenoid valve block. Symptom is a progressively softer brake lever over 200-500 miles with no external leak evidence. The ABS warning lamp illuminates after full pressure loss. Not a safety recall — covered by Tier 2 warranty extension.
fix_procedure: Diagnose with Digital Technician II: perform ABS pressure build test, reject if below 2100 psi in 6 seconds. Replace HCU (part 41700053A for 2014-2016, 41700094 for 2017+). Bleed entire system using DOT 4 brake fluid starting at the HCU bleed ports. Reset ABS calibration via DTC. Labor time: 3.8 hours including system bleed.
verified_by: HD Service Bulletin M-1345 / XL Forum archive 2019-11

title: Cam chain tensioner shoe wear inspection procedure
description: Spring-loaded hydraulic cam chain tensioners in rubber-mount Sportster engines develop accelerated shoe wear past 40,000 miles, leading to cam chain rattle on cold start and eventual catastrophic failure if not addressed. Shoe material degrades faster in ethanol-blend fuel regions due to residual fuel vapor in the primary case crankcase vent.
fix_procedure: Remove cam cover per Service Manual Chapter 3. Inspect inner and outer shoes: replace if shoe thickness measures below 0.160 inches at the wear face. Install revised shoe kit 25284-99B (updated high-temp compound). Torque cam cover bolts to 120 in-lbs using revised tightening sequence in bulletin diagram. Reset service counter in ECM after repair. Labor: 2.2 hours.
verified_by: HD Service Bulletin M-1267 / XLForum confirmed

title: Reflex Linked Brake pulsation caused by rotor runout
description: Street Glide and Road Glide variants with Reflex Defensive Rider Systems may develop brake pedal pulsation between 35-55 mph caused by rear rotor lateral runout exceeding 0.006 inches. The lateral loading from the reflex linking solenoid amplifies existing runout into a noticeable feedback pattern. Not a recall — appearance condition addressed under extended service.
fix_procedure: Measure rear rotor runout with dial indicator at outer edge: maximum acceptable 0.004 inches. If exceeded, replace rotor (part 41500019 for solid disc, 41500028 for cross-drilled). Torque rotor bolts to 23 ft-lbs using crossing pattern with Loctite 262. Bed new pads per bulletin: six medium-pressure stops from 30 mph, cool for 10 minutes, repeat. Labor: 1.1 hours.

title: Rear isolator bushing replacement interval reduction
description: Rear engine isolator bushings on Dyna platform compress past specification between 30,000 and 45,000 miles, causing drivetrain lash at low rpm, primary chain wear acceleration, and belt tracking deviation. Original 60,000 mile replacement interval superseded by this bulletin's 35,000 mile recommendation.
fix_procedure: Inspect isolator bushing compression with torque wrench on isolator stud: target value 45 ft-lbs at 0.100 inch compression. Replace paired bushings (part 16209-04A outer, 16198-04A inner) when compression drops below 35 ft-lbs. Use genuine bushings only — aftermarket compressed-rubber variants fail within 8,000 miles. Labor: 2.7 hours. Includes belt tension verification.

title: Infotainment head unit audio crackle software update
description: Boom! Box GTS infotainment units on 2020-2024 Road Glide trims may produce audio crackle or pop on Bluetooth call termination and when switching between FM and streaming sources. Root cause identified as audio buffer race condition in head unit firmware revisions below 2.3.1.0003.
fix_procedure: Check current firmware version via Settings > System > About. If below 2.3.1.0003, connect USB stick with latest firmware image downloaded from HD service portal. Installation is user-initiated through Settings > System > Update. Duration 22 minutes. Do not interrupt or remove USB during flash. Verify audio on all four speaker zones after update. Labor: 0.4 hours.

title: Rear shock preload adjuster cable binding fix
description: Rear shock remote preload adjuster on 2018-2021 Softail Heritage and Deluxe variants may bind in mid-travel position after approximately 15,000 miles, caused by moisture ingress through the handlebar cable routing gland. Manifests as adjuster knob turning freely without corresponding shock firmness change.
fix_procedure: Remove right-side handlebar switch housing. Inspect adjuster cable entry gland for corrosion. Replace cable assembly (part 54000241) if adjuster fails to achieve full travel range. Apply Harley-Davidson Cable Lube 99862-00 at both cable ends during reassembly. Torque switch housing screws to 25 in-lbs. Verify adjuster travel: minimum 4 full knob rotations between lightest and firmest positions. Labor: 1.4 hours.

title: Oil cooler mounting bracket fatigue crack inspection
description: Rubber-mount Sportster XL883 and XL1200 models built between 2004 and 2013 may develop hairline fatigue cracks in the oil cooler mounting bracket at the primary-case attachment point. Crack propagates under vibration, eventually failing and allowing the cooler to contact frame crossmember. Early detection prevents oil loss and frame damage.
fix_procedure: Annual visual inspection of bracket (part 63042-04) around all four bolt holes and the bend radius beneath the cooler body. Apply penetrating dye if unsure. Replace bracket with revised design 63042-04A (thicker gauge material, improved bend radius) if any crack detected. Torque to 120 in-lbs in four-step pattern specified in bulletin. Labor: 0.9 hours including dye-check.
verified_by: HD Service Bulletin M-1356 / XLForum 2018 oil cooler thread

title: Adaptive Ride Height solenoid water ingress remediation
description: Pan America 1250 Special trims equipped with Adaptive Ride Height (ARH) may develop a stuck-low condition after exposure to pressure washing or heavy rain, caused by water intrusion past the front solenoid dust boot. The system fault stores DTC U1A23 but does not illuminate a warning lamp until ignition cycle.
fix_procedure: Scan for U1A23. Remove front fork ARH solenoid (part 46000485). Inspect internal O-ring (item 8 in exploded view) for moisture contamination and corrosion. Clean with electronics contact cleaner. Replace dust boot (part 46000490) with revised updated part 46000490A which includes secondary lip seal. Bleed ARH system via service tool. Relearn calibration. Labor: 1.8 hours.

title: Voltage regulator connector pin fretting corrosion
description: 2009-2013 Touring models with 50-amp voltage regulators experience intermittent charging faults traced to pin fretting corrosion inside the six-pin regulator connector. Fretting is accelerated by aftermarket pigtail adapters and by route exposure to road spray. Symptom: battery warning on dashboard, voltage reads 12.6V steady at 3000 rpm instead of 14.2V.
fix_procedure: Disconnect battery negative terminal. Unplug regulator connector. Inspect six male pins for green oxidation or black fretting residue. Clean with NO-OX-ID A Special grease applied sparingly with cotton swab. Do NOT use dielectric grease — it traps corrosive vapors. Reseat connector with audible click. If pin tension is loose, replace pigtail 72403-01. Verify charging: 13.8-14.5V at 3000 rpm. Labor: 0.6 hours.
verified_by: HD Service Bulletin M-1178 / XLForum electrical archive

title: Fuel pump pressure regulator replacement for hot-restart issue
description: 2018-2021 Gold Wing GL1800 (redesigned platform) may fail to restart when hot, caused by residual fuel pressure drop below 48 psi after 30-minute heat soak. Root cause identified as fuel pressure regulator spring relaxation in the first-production pump assembly. Symptom is extended cranking (6-12 seconds) before start when engine is hot.
fix_procedure: Verify condition with Honda Diagnostic System fuel pressure scan: minimum 50 psi at key-on, hot. If below, replace pump module (part 16700-MKC-A01) with revised assembly 16700-MKC-A02 which includes upgraded regulator spring and improved pump inlet strainer. Prime system three times with key-on before first start. Labor time: 1.8 hours.

title: Quickshifter sensor calibration procedure update
description: CBR1000RR SP and SP2 with factory quickshifter may exhibit inconsistent upshift engagement between 3rd and 4th gears, caused by position sensor drift after high-rpm track use. Replaces procedure in Service Manual 61MKB01: calibration now requires cold-engine baseline plus hot-engine verification, not just cold-only.
fix_procedure: Connect HDS. Navigate to Shifter Calibration > Position Learn. Follow three-phase procedure: (1) cold engine, key-on engine-off: cycle gear lever through 1-2-3-4-5-6-N, each hold 2 seconds; (2) warm engine idle 10 minutes, repeat cycle; (3) test ride 5 miles with deliberate clean upshifts at 7000-9000 rpm. If DTC P2227 persists, replace shift sensor assembly 56200-MKC-A00. Labor: 0.7 hours.

title: DCT oil filter bypass valve revision
description: 2016-2019 CRF1000L Africa Twin DCT models may exhibit delayed gear engagement when starting from cold below 30°F ambient, caused by the original DCT oil filter bypass valve sticking closed at low temperatures. Updated valve uses dual-spring design for more consistent cold-weather operation.
fix_procedure: Drain DCT oil per Service Manual Chapter 4. Remove DCT oil filter housing (4 bolts). Replace bypass valve assembly 15412-MJP-G51 with revised 15412-MJP-G52. Install new filter 15412-MGS-D21. Refill with Honda HP4M or equivalent 10W-30 synthetic, capacity 4.3 liters. Run through full DCT adaptation: idle 10 min, then ride with clutch calibration learning enabled. Labor: 1.5 hours.

title: Intake manifold boot crack monitoring — elevated humidity climates
description: CB500F, CB500X, CBR500R models operated in high-humidity coastal climates may experience accelerated cracking of the rubber intake manifold boots between 25,000 and 40,000 miles. Cracks develop at the carburetor-side clamp boss and cause small but persistent vacuum leaks manifesting as rough idle and lean fault codes.
fix_procedure: With engine cold, spray isopropyl alcohol around boot seams while listening for idle rpm change. Replace cracked boots with revised part 16211-MKA-D00 which uses fluorosilicone compound resistant to salt-air degradation. Torque clamps to 24 in-lbs. Recheck idle stability after 50-mile break-in. Part 16211-MKA-D00 supersedes 16211-MKA-D01. Labor: 1.2 hours including throttle body cleaning.

title: Front fork seal seepage at 10,000 mile threshold
description: VFR1200F and VFR1200X Crosstourer models develop premature front fork seal weepage between 8,000 and 12,000 miles caused by inner tube chrome finish variation at production. Affected serial range is VIN positions 2,000-4,800 within model year. Updated seals include improved dust wiper geometry.
fix_procedure: Inspect fork seal area at every oil change. When first seepage appears, clean inner tube with plastic business card edge to remove dust residue behind dust wiper. If seep persists after two rides, replace paired seal kit 51490-MGE-003 with revised 51490-MGE-013. Use genuine Honda fork oil SS-47 Ultra-G, capacity 528 cc per leg at 140 mm air gap. Labor: 2.3 hours both sides.

title: Idle air control valve carbon buildup cleaning protocol
description: CMX500 and CMX300 Rebel models driven primarily on short trips under 8 miles may accumulate carbon deposits on the idle air control valve, causing hanging idle (rpm drops slowly to target instead of returning promptly) and occasional stall at stoplights. Concentration highest in urban commuting use patterns.
fix_procedure: Remove throttle body assembly per Service Manual Chapter 5. Clean IAC valve opening and seat with CRC MAF Sensor Cleaner — do NOT use carburetor cleaner (damages coating). Inspect for throttle plate carbon: clean with soft nylon brush. Replace throttle body gasket 16210-MJP-000. Perform IAC relearn: key-on 5 seconds, key-off, repeat three times, then start and idle 15 minutes. Labor: 1.3 hours.

title: Slipper clutch pack torque specification revision
description: 2007-2012 CBR600RR slipper clutch assembly may exhibit chatter under hard deceleration if center hub nut torque drifts below specification. Original service manual specified 93 ft-lbs — revised to 103 ft-lbs with updated locking washer (part 22361-MFJ-D00) to prevent rotation at elevated rpm braking loads experienced on track.
fix_procedure: Remove clutch cover. Bend back locking tab on washer. Loosen center hub nut. Replace washer with revised 22361-MFJ-D00 (thicker gauge, dual locking tabs). Retorque center hub nut to 103 ft-lbs while holding clutch basket with Honda service tool 07CMB-MEJA100 or equivalent. Bend both locking tabs into nut flats. Reinstall cover with new gasket 11394-MFJ-000. Labor: 1.1 hours.

title: Clutch basket rivet inspection at 18,000 mile interval
description: MSX125 Grom clutch basket rivets may loosen between 15,000 and 22,000 miles in aggressive-shift riding patterns (track day use, aftermarket gearing). Loose rivets cause clutch chatter, difficult neutral finding when stopped, and clutch slip at peak torque rpm. Early replacement prevents basket-to-crankshaft fretting damage.
fix_procedure: Inspect rivets visually through clutch access port — look for metallic dust rings around each of the six drive-plate rivets. Measure lateral play of clutch basket: maximum 0.004 inches. If exceeded or dust present, replace complete clutch basket 22100-K26-900 (includes pre-riveted plate assembly). Do NOT attempt rivet replacement — alignment tolerance exceeds field repair capability. Labor: 1.4 hours.

title: Exhaust heat shield rattle at specific rpm band
description: 2019-2023 Z125 Monkey models may develop exhaust heat shield rattle between 3800 and 4400 rpm caused by mounting grommet compression set after thermal cycling. Rattle does not indicate exhaust damage and does not affect performance but is noticeable at cruise speeds on smooth pavement.
fix_procedure: Remove three M6 bolts securing heat shield. Replace three rubber grommets (part 18355-K26-902 — retainer sold separately) with revised silicone-core version 18355-K26-912. Reinstall shield with updated grommets using 4 ft-lbs torque. Do not overtighten — excess torque returns rattle by compressing new grommets. Labor: 0.3 hours.

title: DCT solenoid harness chafe location repair
description: NC700X DCT models may develop chafe wear on the DCT solenoid wiring harness where it routes behind the throttle body bracket, caused by handlebar-side vibration transmission through the upper frame rail. Chafe manifests as intermittent DCT fault codes P0872/P0877 during transitions between gears.
fix_procedure: Scan for P0872, P0877 DTCs. Access harness route per Service Manual Chapter 8. Inspect section of harness from throttle body bracket forward 6 inches. If chafe present, wrap damaged section with 3M Super 33+ electrical tape extending 1 inch beyond both ends of wear area. Install updated chafe guard 32422-MJN-900 at throttle body bracket. Reroute harness forward of the bracket tab, not behind. Labor: 0.8 hours.

title: Transmission dog engagement surface heat treatment update
description: 2015-2019 YZF-R1 may exhibit 2nd gear jumpout (transmission selects neutral under load between 2nd and 3rd) after track use exceeding 8,000 miles. Root cause identified as variable heat treatment depth on 2nd/3rd dog engagement surfaces in early-production transmission shafts. Updated gearset uses revised carburizing process.
fix_procedure: Verify jumpout by full-throttle dyno run or track test with data acquisition. If jumpout occurs above 9,500 rpm under load, split engine cases per Service Manual section 5. Replace mainshaft assembly with updated gearset part 2CR-17411-10 (supersedes 2CR-17411-00). Do NOT reuse original 2nd/3rd gear pair. Torque case bolts in 8-stage pattern per bulletin diagram. Labor: 18 hours including engine R&R.

title: Fly-by-wire throttle grip sensor dead band elimination
description: MT-09 and derivative models (XSR900, Tracer 900) may develop a slight throttle dead band in the first 10 degrees of grip rotation past closed, creating a perception of throttle lag off-closed in stop-and-go traffic. Firmware update narrows the dead band without affecting off-idle stability.
fix_procedure: Connect Yamaha Diagnostic Tool. Check current ECU firmware via Active Diagnosis > Main Unit. If firmware ending in revision -08 or below, flash to revision -11 or later. Duration: 7 minutes. After flash, perform TPS reset: key-on, hold grip fully open for 10 seconds, release, key-off. Restart and test throttle response in first-gear rollon. Labor: 0.5 hours.

title: Electric shift YCC-S clutch adapter bolt retorque
description: FJR1300AE/AS models with Yamaha Chip Controlled Shift may experience occasional missed shifts above 6,000 rpm caused by clutch adapter bolt retention loss. The torque-to-yield bolt relaxes approximately 8% after 15,000 miles of mixed use. Retorque to revised spec restores normal shift quality.
fix_procedure: Remove clutch cover per Service Manual section 3. Loosen clutch adapter bolt (part 90104-12095). Apply Three-Bond 1322 anaerobic sealant to threads. Retorque to revised spec: 88 ft-lbs (was 74 ft-lbs). Do NOT reuse original bolt — replace with 90104-12105 which has updated head geometry. Reinstall cover with new O-ring 93210-99010. Labor: 1.3 hours.

title: Steering head bearing preload adjustment revision
description: Tenere 700 models may develop slight steering head notchiness at the straight-ahead position after 8,000-12,000 miles, caused by insufficient preload at assembly and subsequent bearing race indexing. Revised preload spec addresses this plus eases initial steering input during slow-speed maneuvering.
fix_procedure: Support bike on center stand. Remove top steering nut. Loosen stem nut. Measure current preload torque: specification now 11 ft-lbs (was 7 ft-lbs). Cycle steering lock-to-lock 10 times. Recheck preload — retorque to 11 ft-lbs if dropped. Reassemble with new steering stem cap nut 90170-20015 torqued to 78 ft-lbs. Test figure-eight at walking pace to verify smooth response. Labor: 1.5 hours.

title: V-Max YCC-I intake butterfly actuator replacement
description: 2009-2017 VMAX variable intake system (YCC-I) may exhibit reduced top-end power delivery beyond 8,000 rpm caused by butterfly actuator wear at 25,000+ miles. Partial actuator travel limits intake runner resonance-tuning effect. Symptom often missed because power loss is gradual and 8,000+ rpm use is infrequent.
fix_procedure: With Yamaha Diagnostic Tool, command full actuator sweep via Active Test > YCC-I Function. Observe butterfly positions visually with air cleaner removed. If either butterfly fails to achieve 85 degrees open, replace actuator assembly 2S3-13950-10 (supersedes 2S3-13950-00). Calibrate per service procedure: key-on 30 seconds allows ECU self-learn. Labor: 3.5 hours including airbox R&R.

title: Rear brake master cylinder piston seal service kit
description: 2015-2022 MT-07 and FZ-07 models may develop soft rear brake lever feel around 20,000 miles caused by primary seal hardening in the rear master cylinder. Seal compound replaced in late-2022 production with improved low-temperature flexibility formulation.
fix_procedure: Drain rear brake reservoir. Remove master cylinder piston assembly per Service Manual section 4. Install service kit 1WS-W0041-00 (includes primary/secondary seals, piston, boot, and new bolt). Torque master cylinder bolts to 10 ft-lbs using crossing pattern. Bleed rear brake: lever feel should firm by 6th pump cycle. Use DOT 4 brake fluid only. Labor: 1.1 hours.

title: ECU mapping revision for mid-rpm throttle response surge
description: 2014-2016 FZ-09 exhibits a noticeable torque surge between 3,500 and 4,500 rpm in A-mode, perceived by riders as abrupt power delivery in corner exits. Revised ECU map smooths the transition without reducing peak power. Applies only to A-mode; STD and B modes unchanged.
fix_procedure: Connect Yamaha Diagnostic Tool. Verify current ECU calibration in System Info screen. If ending in -04 or -06, update to -09 (covers all three modes). Flash duration 9 minutes. Cycle ignition off for 60 seconds after completion. Test A-mode with controlled rollon from 3,000 rpm to 5,500 rpm in 2nd gear — surge should be imperceptible. Labor: 0.6 hours.
verified_by: Yamaha Technical Bulletin TB-2016-117 / FZ-09 forum archive

title: Cooling fan activation threshold adjustment for urban use
description: 2022-2024 YZF-R7 operated primarily in urban stop-and-go conditions may experience coolant temperature excursions above 110°C at prolonged idle, caused by fan activation threshold set for mixed-use riding rather than continuous-idle scenarios. Firmware update lowers the fan-on point by 3°C and extends fan-off delay by 20 seconds.
fix_procedure: Connect Yamaha Diagnostic Tool. Verify current ECU version in System Info. If below revision -05, flash to revision -07 or later. Duration 6 minutes. After flash, run engine at idle until fan cycles at least twice — fan should activate by 103°C and deactivate by 97°C. If fan fails to cycle within 20 minutes of idle, escalate to thermistor circuit diagnosis. Labor: 0.5 hours.

title: Kawasaki quick shifter strain gauge calibration drift
description: 2016-2020 Ninja ZX-10R with factory quickshifter (KQS) may exhibit shift fork damage after repeated failed upshifts caused by strain gauge calibration drift past 15,000 miles. Drift shifts the ignition-cut trigger threshold upward, requiring harder shifter pressure than the gearbox dogs can tolerate at high rpm.
fix_procedure: Scan KQS calibration via Kawasaki Diagnostic System. Baseline value should read 0.85-1.10 V at rest. If outside range, perform strain gauge recalibration: key-on engine-off, hold shifter at neutral for 30 seconds, release. If recalibration fails to stabilize reading, replace shift rod assembly with strain gauge 13236-0660. Inspect shift forks for edge wear — replace if radius exceeds 0.5 mm. Labor: 2.1 hours for calibration only, 6+ hours if forks damaged.

title: Radiator mounting rubber isolator revised specification
description: Versys 650 and Versys 1000 models may develop radiator fan vibration complaint at extended idle (stop-and-go traffic over 20 minutes) caused by rubber isolator hardening at the lower radiator mount. Revised isolator uses dual-durometer compound for better vibration damping without reducing support.
fix_procedure: Drain coolant below lower radiator mount level. Remove two lower mount bolts (M8, torque 19 ft-lbs). Replace two rubber isolators (part 92075-1727) with revised 92075-1727A. Reinstall mount bolts with Loctite 243. Top off coolant — Kawasaki Permanent Coolant long-life formula, total system capacity 2.2 quarts. Bleed via bleeder screw on thermostat housing. Labor: 1.0 hour.

title: Fuel tank internal coating delamination inspection
description: 2012-2018 Ninja 650 and related parallel-twin platforms may experience internal fuel tank coating delamination in units stored with ethanol-blend fuel for extended periods (over 6 months) without a stabilizer. Delaminated coating flakes clog the fuel pump strainer and cause fuel starvation symptoms ranging from mild stumble to complete no-start.
fix_procedure: Remove fuel pump assembly per Service Manual Chapter 4. Inspect pump strainer and in-tank sock filter for black or grey flakes. Inspect inside of tank with borescope through pump aperture — coating should appear uniform beige. If delamination visible or flakes present in pump assembly: replace fuel pump 49040-0760 AND send tank for professional recoating (or replace entire tank 51002-0700). Labor: 2.0 hours diagnostic + repair scope varies.
verified_by: Kawasaki Service Bulletin SB-2017-022 / Ninja 650 forum

title: New-gen KLR650 fuel injection warmup stumble map revision
description: 2022+ KLR650 (new-generation fuel-injected platform) may exhibit cold-start stumbling and hesitation between cold start and 3 minutes of idle, particularly in ambient temperatures below 45°F. Revised fuel map increases enrichment during closed-loop transition phase. Not a defect — map revision addresses field feedback.
fix_procedure: Connect Kawasaki Diagnostic System. Check ECU calibration version in Unit Info screen. If version ending 02-05, update to version 08 or later. Flash takes 8 minutes. After flash, start engine cold — should idle clean to target rpm within 20 seconds of start. Do not interrupt flash or power-cycle during update. Labor: 0.5 hours.

title: KIPASS antenna coil sensitivity degradation
description: Concours 14 (ZG1400) with KIPASS keyless ignition may experience intermittent no-crank condition after 8 years of age caused by antenna coil sensitivity degradation inside the ignition switch housing. Symptom often appears in cold weather and resolves temporarily when key fob is placed directly against the switch housing.
fix_procedure: Verify condition: with fob battery fresh (test with multimeter — minimum 3.0V), attempt start from normal pocket position. If no-crank but works when fob pressed against switch, replace ignition switch assembly with antenna 27005-0614. Program new switch to ECU via Kawasaki Diagnostic System — requires Master PIN. Save both fob IDs before replacement. Labor: 2.8 hours plus KIPASS programming.

title: Clutch friction plate bonding procedure update
description: 2017-2019 Z900 clutch friction plate bonding may fail after high-temperature operation, typically after 10+ minute high-load track sessions. Manifests as clutch slip under aggressive acceleration and metallic debris in gearbox oil at subsequent oil change. Revised plates use upgraded adhesive compound.
fix_procedure: Drain engine oil. Remove clutch cover per Service Manual Chapter 3. Disassemble clutch pack. Inspect friction plates for material debonding (visible gaps between friction material and steel backing). If any plate shows debonding, replace complete clutch plate kit 13088-0070 (supersedes 13088-0067) which includes updated bonding compound. Also replace clutch basket if blue-tinted from overheating. Labor: 2.5 hours.

title: Front brake caliper piston dust seal service kit update
description: 2011-2015 GSX-R600 and GSX-R750 front brake caliper piston dust seals may fail prematurely (12,000 to 18,000 miles) in high-use touring applications. Failure manifests as intermittent caliper drag after extended slow-speed city riding. Revised seal kit includes higher-temperature silicone dust boot.
fix_procedure: Remove calipers per Service Manual Chapter 5. Disassemble pistons using compressed air in the bleed port (CAUTION: pistons eject rapidly). Replace dust seals (updated part 59186-00B01) and piston seals. Clean caliper bore with isopropyl alcohol. Lubricate new piston seals lightly with Suzuki Red Rubber Grease. Reassemble and bleed with DOT 4. Labor: 2.0 hours both sides.

title: Low-rpm stumble at altitudes above 6,000 feet
description: 2017-2021 V-Strom 650 and 1000 models may exhibit low-rpm stumble and occasional stall at altitudes above 6,000 feet caused by fuel-trim correction limits in ECU firmware. Revised firmware expands fuel trim authority by 8% to maintain drivability in mountainous terrain without affecting sea-level emissions compliance.
fix_procedure: Connect Suzuki Diagnostic Tool SDT-IV. Verify ECU firmware revision. If below revision 1.34, flash to 1.41 or later. Duration 11 minutes with battery on charger. After flash, perform ISC reset: key-on engine-off hold throttle open 10 seconds, release, key-off 30 seconds. Start and idle 20 minutes at sea-level elevation (if available) for baseline re-learn. Labor: 0.7 hours.

title: Carb synchronization procedure clarification for altitude variance
description: GSF600/1200 Bandit carbureted models display wider-than-expected idle synchronization drift between service intervals when the bike is moved across significant altitude changes. Revised service procedure adds intermediate-altitude sync check, superseding older Service Manual Chapter 5 procedure.
fix_procedure: Warm engine to full operating temperature (2 riding miles minimum). Install manometer on all four carburetor vacuum ports. With engine at 1,100 rpm idle, measure vacuum at each port — all four must read within 2 cmHg of one another. If out of spec, adjust synchronization screws using Suzuki tool 09913-13121. Retest at 3,000 rpm (no-load) — difference must remain within 3 cmHg. Labor: 1.5 hours.

title: Clutch lever bushing wear accelerated with aftermarket levers
description: 2017-2022 SV650 with aftermarket shorty-lever installations (non-OEM) may experience accelerated bushing wear in the clutch perch — symptom is lever lateral play exceeding 2 mm and progressively heavier clutch pull. OEM lever geometry distributes load correctly; shorter aftermarket levers amplify side-load on the bushing.
fix_procedure: Inspect clutch lever perch for bushing elongation and lateral play. If play exceeds 1.5 mm, replace perch bushing kit 57630-10F00 (includes nylon bushing and pivot bolt). Consider reverting to OEM lever (part 57621-04K00) or confirming aftermarket lever is within 5% of OEM dimensions. Lubricate pivot with Suzuki Super Grease. Labor: 0.4 hours.

title: Semi-active WP suspension valve calibration drift
description: 1290 Super Adventure R/S and Super Duke R models with WP Semi-Active Suspension may experience valve calibration drift after approximately 12,000 miles resulting in noticeable compression damping inconsistency between left and right fork legs. Dealer recalibration procedure restores factory balance without component replacement in most cases.
fix_procedure: Connect KTM Diagnostic Tool (K-Dia-T). Run Semi-Active Suspension Diagnostic > Valve Calibration routine. Procedure requires bike on rear stand with front wheel unloaded. Duration 15 minutes including valve cycling. If calibration fails to converge within 3 attempts, replace affected valve cartridge (left/right specific parts 79201134001/79201135001). Labor: 1.0 hour calibration only, 4+ hours if cartridge replacement needed.

title: LC8c engine oil consumption at break-in
description: 790 Duke and Adventure (LC8c parallel-twin) may consume up to 0.4 liter of oil per 1,000 km during the break-in period (first 1,500 km), which is higher than mechanics and owners expect relative to KTM singles. This is normal during piston-ring seating and does NOT indicate engine defect. Consumption stabilizes below 0.1 l/1000 km after break-in complete.
fix_procedure: Verify consumption by checking oil level at consistent conditions (cold, bike vertical, 2 minutes after shutoff). Document readings. If consumption persists beyond 3,000 km at elevated rate, perform compression test all cylinders: minimum 195 psi each, differential below 10%. If within spec, no repair required. Inform owner consumption is normal during seating. If outside spec, escalate to engine internals diagnosis. Labor: 0.8 hours diagnostic only.

title: Counter-balancer tensioner check valve service update
description: 2016-2022 690 Duke and Enduro R (LC4 single) may develop counter-balancer rattle on warm restart after fuel tank runs low below 1.5 liters. Root cause is balancer tensioner check valve bleeding back pressure during tank-starve events. Updated valve assembly restores pressure retention during short low-fuel intervals.
fix_procedure: Drain engine oil. Remove right engine case cover (20 fasteners). Replace balancer chain tensioner assembly 78036003044 with updated 78036003144 which includes revised check valve. Reuse existing chain and sprockets if within service limits. Reassemble with new case gasket 78030046000. Refill with Motorex Power Synt 4T 10W-60 (4.0 quart wet-sump capacity including filter). Labor: 3.5 hours.

title: Throttle cable routing at fork compression limits
description: 2015-2019 RC390 race-derived sport bike may experience throttle pull-back at full fork compression during heavy track-braking, caused by throttle cable routing that stretches when the steering head rotates under fork bottoming. Symptom is unexpected throttle blip during corner entry when cornering hard with fork compressed.
fix_procedure: Reroute throttle cable per updated routing diagram in bulletin. Cable now passes over the left fork leg with a service loop at the steering head instead of behind the fork. Replace cable with longer updated part 90112090033 (supersedes 90112090028 which is 40mm shorter). Verify full fork compression with steering at full lock both directions — cable must remain slack at all positions. Labor: 1.2 hours.
